title: oxalis peduncularis var. pilosa
intro: Oxalis peduncularis var. pilosa, commonly known as the hairy woodsorrel, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Oxalidaceae family. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of arthritis due to its high concentration of oxalic acid and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, and is also believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help promote overall health and well-being. Oxalis peduncularis var. pilosa can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal medicine to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal issues.
